generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance CraneCartMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Stepper motor (TMC4671-Eval controller) for horizontal movement of the crane carriage"
]

instance CraneHoistMotor : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Stepper motor (TMC4671-Eval controller) for vertical lifting of the hoist"
]

instance CraneElectromagnet : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Actuates to pick up and release containers"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the angular position of the hoist swing for feedback control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleData
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures wind speed to influence crane stability (indirectly sensed via Arduino)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData WindSpeedData
]

instance ArduinoHub : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Central hub for reading sensor data (swing encoder, anemometer) and interfacing with motor controllers"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SensorHubData
]

// C3: Produced Data from Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance WindSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

instance SensorHubData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ArduinoHub
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction Data Flows
instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleData, SensorHubData
]

instance VelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SensorHubData
]

instance AccelerationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SensorHubData
]

instance WindInfluenceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom WindSpeedData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CADCraneFrame : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Autodesk Fusion 360 model of the crane frame"
]

instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Used by the Trajectory Generator Service to compute optimal movement paths with minimal residual swinging motion"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance ArcadiaSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "System engineering models (e.g., for teaching purposes) using Arcadia/Capella notation"
]

// C10: Time-Series Data
instance CraneStateLog : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous logging of crane state (position, velocity, sensor data) to TimescaleDB"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, VelocityData, AccelerationData, WindInfluenceData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LoggingApplication
]

instance TrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Generated trajectories with minimal residual swinging motion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData KinematicModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationService
]

instance SimulationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulation data generated by the Simulation Service for comparison against real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrajectoryData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance LoggingApplication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based logging application that writes crane data to TimescaleDB"
    DTDFVocab:enables TimescaleDBService
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker for service communication between crane components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamService
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical data visualization dashboard"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DockerContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Containerization platform for deploying TimescaleDB, MQTT broker, and Grafana via docker-compose"
    DTDFVocab:enables TimescaleDBService, MQTTBroker, GrafanaDashboard
]

instance PythonEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core programming environment using Python for control applications, logging, and services"
    DTDFVocab:enables LoggingApplication, TrajectoryGeneratorService, ContinuousValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses a kinematic model to generate trajectories with minimal residual swinging motion"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Compares real-time crane data with simulated trajectories to detect deviations and notify operators"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAlertAction
]

instance DataStreamService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Handles real-time data streaming via MQTT broker between components"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataLoggingAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time and historical data visualization via Grafana Dashboard"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataVisualizationInsight
]

instance ControlApplication : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Transmits operator inputs (trajectory commands) to physical crane via Python"
    DTDFVocab:provides CommandExecutionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides optimal movement paths with minimal residual swinging motion, improving crane efficiency"
]

instance DeviationAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Notifies operators via Grafana Dashboard when trajectory deviations exceed predefined thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataLoggingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuously logs crane state data to TimescaleDB via Python Logging Application"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides real-time and historical visualization of crane operational data"
]

instance CommandExecutionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Executes operator commands (trajectory movements) on the physical crane via Control Application"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Physical Crane System
instance PhysicalCraneSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lab-scale gantry crane system inspired by harbor cranes in the port of Antwerp, designed to move small pucks between predefined locations"
    base:contains CraneCartMotor, CraneHoistMotor, CraneElectromagnet, SwingAngleEncoder, ArduinoHub
]

// C1: Digital Twin System
instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Unified virtual representation of the physical crane system comprising database, services, and visualization components"
    base:contains TimescaleDBService, MQTTBroker, GrafanaDashboard, TrajectoryGeneratorService, ContinuousValidationService
]

// C1: Centralized Host Environment
instance CentralizedHost : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalCraneSystem, DigitalTwinSystem
    base:desc "Centralized architecture where all physical and virtual components operate on the same host via Docker containers"
]

// C1: TimescaleDB Service
instance TimescaleDBService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Time-series database for storing crane data (position, velocity, sensor readings, trajectories)"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataStorageInsight
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LoggingApplication, TrajectoryData, SimulationData
]

instance DataStorageInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Ensures all crane operational data is persistently stored in structured time-series format"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The digital twin interacts with the physical system through:
  - **Trajectory Generation Service**: Sends optimized movement trajectories (kinematic model-based) to the **Control Application** for execution by the physical crane.
  - **Continuous Validation Service**: Compares real-time sensor data with simulated trajectories to detect deviations; alerts operators via the **Dashboard** if thresholds are exceeded.
  - **Simulation Service**: Provides simulated trajectories and confidence bounds (upper/lower bounds) for validation purposes, which are visualized on the **Grafana Dashboard**."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** scale:
  - **Data Logging**: Continuous, near-instantaneous updates to **TimescaleDB** (latency not specified but assumed sub-second).
  - **Trajectory Generation**: Computed offline or in real-time during operation (kinematic model-based).
  - **Continuous Validation**: Runs in real-time, comparing streaming sensor data with simulated trajectories with a specified threshold (not quantified but implied to be adaptive)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is orchestrated as follows:
  - **Centralized Architecture**: All services (database, MQTT broker, dashboard, services) run on a single host (personal computer).
  - **Service Interactions**:
    - **Logging Application** → **TimescaleDB** (via Python).
    - **Control Application** ↔ **Physical Crane Interface** (Python-Physical).
    - **Trajectory Generator Service** → **Simulation Service** (kinematic model-based).
    - **Continuous Validation Service** ↔ **Dashboard** (MQTT broker for alerts).
    - **Human Machine Interface** ↔ **Control Application** (operator commands).
  - **Data Flow**: Unidirectional from physical to digital (logging, validation) and bidirectional for control (trajectories, commands)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates across the following lifecycle stages of the physical system:
  - **As Designed**: Kinematic models (CAD/Capella) represent idealized crane behavior.
  - **As Manufactured**: Physical crane hardware and controller calibration (real-world deviations).
  - **As Operated**: Real-time data logging, validation, and optimization during runtime."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - **Continuous Validation Service**: Compares real-time sensor data with simulated trajectories using distance metrics (threshold-based alerts).
    - **Kinematic Model**: Used for trajectory generation (no explicit error bounds mentioned but implied to be calibrated).
  - **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Simulation Service**: Provides confidence bounds (upper/lower) for trajectory traces (visualized on Grafana).
    - **Sensor Data**: Logged to TimescaleDB for post-hoc analysis.
  - **Verification/Validation**:
    - **Capella Models**: Used for teaching and system engineering validation.
    - **Real-Time Feedback**: Operators receive alerts if validation thresholds are breached."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Technical connections between the physical twin and digital twin include:
  - **Network Protocols**:
    - **MQTT (Eclipse Mosquitto)**: Used for service-to-service communication.
    - **HTTP/REST**: Likely used for database interactions (TimescaleDB).
  - **Data Transmission**:
    - **Logging Application**: Python script sends sensor data to TimescaleDB.
    - **Control Application**: Python script sends trajectory commands to physical crane.
  - **Hardware Interfaces**:
    - **Arduino**: Acts as a bridge between sensors (swing encoder, anemometer) and motor controllers."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is hosted and deployed as follows:
  - **Hosting Environment**: Runs on a **personal computer** (single-node setup).
  - **Containerization**: Database, MQTT broker, and dashboard are containerized via **Docker** (using `docker-compose` for orchestration).
  - **Initialization**: Docker setup automates database and dashboard initialization during first-time deployment.
  - **Software Stack**:
    - **Python**: Core runtime for all services (`gantrylib` module).
    - **TimescaleDB**: PostgreSQL-based time-series database.
    - **Grafana**: Open-source visualization tool."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration with external systems is **not explicitly detailed** in the documents. The exemplar focuses internally on the lab-scale gantry crane and its digital twin services, without mention of external system interoperability (e.g., container identification systems, harbor simulations)."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are managed as follows:
  - **Data Ownership**: The **physical crane owner** owns the sensor data logged to **TimescaleDB** (no explicit mention of third-party ownership).
  - **Privacy Considerations**: No explicit mention of data encryption, anonymization, or access controls. The exemplar is open-source, implying public access to data (unless restricted by institutional policies)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Standardization efforts include:
  - **Open-Source Tools**: Use of open-source software (TimescaleDB, Grafana, Mosquitto, Python).
  - **Protocols**: MQTT for service communication (standardized).
  - **Modeling Standards**:
    - **CAD**: Autodesk Fusion 360 (proprietary but widely used).
    - **System Engineering**: Capella (industry-standard for MBSE).
  - **Ad-Hoc Approach**: No commercial digital twin frameworks (e.g., Eclipse Ditto/BaSyx) are used; instead, a custom ad-hoc architecture is employed."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
  - **Remote Operation**: The digital twin can execute commands remotely on the physical crane (e.g., via HMI or MQTT), but **no explicit mention of human-in-the-loop safety protocols** (e.g., emergency stop mechanisms).
  - **Data Integrity**: Continuous validation ensures discrepancies between real and simulated data are flagged, but **no details on cybersecurity measures** (e.g., authentication, encryption) are provided.
  - **Physical Safety**: The crane operates in a controlled lab environment; no mention of industrial safety standards (e.g., ISO 13849) for remote operation."
]
